query: I have a desktop disk-usage widget with :stacking "bg". It shows up fine when I log in, but as soon as I change my wallpaper with swww it disappears. My other widgets are fine. Why?
expected_behavior: Routes to POSITIONING.md (Stacking Layer vs swaybg section)

Explains that eww's 'bg' stacking and the wallpaper daemon (swaybg/swww/wpaperd) share the wlr-layer-shell 'background' layer, and surfaces stack in creation order — the new wallpaper surface lands on top and hides the widget
Does NOT suggest re-opening the widget after every wallpaper change as the fix
Fixes it by switching to :stacking "bottom" so the widget sits above the wallpaper but below normal windows
notes: Guards against the naive 'just reopen the widget' band-aid. The real cause is layer ordering within the shared background layer; only moving to the 'bottom' layer survives a wallpaper change. A model that doesn't know the four-layer ordering would misdiagnose this as an eww bug.
